evidence: src/lib/ cleanly separates into components/, data/, types/, and utils/ — each directory has a clear, non-overlapping responsibility
src/routes/ contains only SvelteKit convention files (+page.svelte, +page.server.ts, +layout.ts, +layout.svelte) with no ad-hoc files
Data files (artefacts.ts, projects.ts, terminal.ts) are pure static declarations with typed exports — no logic, no side effects, predictable ownership
The single-page architecture described in CLAUDE.md matches runtime reality: +page.svelte composes all sections, +page.server.ts fetches supplementary GitHub data
terminal.ts defines its own TerminalFrame interface locally instead of in src/lib/types/index.ts where all other domain types live, creating a minor inconsistency in type ownership
high_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: The TerminalFrame type is defined in terminal.ts rather than in the centralized types/index.ts where Artefact, Project, ProjectGitHubData, and SectionAccent all live. This is a minor decomposition inconsistency: the codebase establishes a clear pattern (domain types in types/index.ts, data in data/) but one file breaks it. Additionally, the three project category exports (impactProjects, explorationProjects, metaProjects) force the page server to know about all three and manually aggregate them, rather than exposing a single allProjects or a unified accessor.

strengths: Directory structure maps cleanly to domain capabilities: components/ for UI, data/ for static content, types/ for contracts, utils/ for API helpers
Public surface is minimal — each data file exports one or two named constants, utils exports two functions, types exports four interfaces/types
CLAUDE.md architecture documentation accurately describes the runtime structure; a new engineer reading it would form correct expectations
Subsystem decomposition localizes change well: adding a new project means editing only data/projects.ts and potentially +page.svelte
Consistent architectural pattern: typed data declarations consumed by Svelte components, with optional GitHub enrichment at build time via server load
high_level_elegance.issue_character: Issues are minor consistency gaps in where types are declared and how project data is aggregated, not structural decomposition failures.
high_level_elegance.score_rationale: The codebase is small (7 TS files, 273 LOC) and demonstrates strong high-level decomposition. A developer can explain why each directory and file exists, ownership boundaries are clear, and the documented architecture matches reality. The score sits at 88 rather than 90+ because of the TerminalFrame type placement inconsistency and the somewhat repetitive project aggregation pattern in +page.server.ts that requires manual awareness of all three category exports. These are minor friction points in an otherwise well-organized structure.

issues.summary: TerminalFrame interface defined in data/terminal.ts instead of centralized types/index.ts
evidence: src/lib/types/index.ts exports Artefact, Project, ProjectGitHubData, and SectionAccent — establishing a clear convention that domain types live here
src/lib/data/artefacts.ts imports its Artefact type from '$lib/types', following the convention
src/lib/data/projects.ts imports its Project type from '$lib/types', following the convention
src/lib/data/terminal.ts defines TerminalFrame locally on line 1 and exports it from the data file, breaking the established type-ownership pattern
issues.suggestion: Move the TerminalFrame interface to src/lib/types/index.ts and import it in terminal.ts, matching the pattern used by artefacts.ts and projects.ts.
